concerns.text: Plan summary and T004 imply a 'synthetic data fallback' strategy. This violates Constitution Principle VII (Materials Data Provenance) which mandates data be obtained 'exclusively' from the cited study and Materials Project API. Introducing synthetic data as a primary pipeline option silently relaxes the provenance constraint.

Task T012 implements only 3 descriptors (δ, Δχ, ΔHmix). The User Story 1 Independent Test and Spec US-1 explicitly require '≥10 computed descriptors'. This task deliverable falls short of the acceptance criteria, constituting a silent weakening of the functional requirement.

Task T025 specifies implementing the 'Nadeau & Bengio corrected t-test'. While scientifically sound, FR-005 explicitly mandates a 'paired t-test'. Without an explicit spec amendment or task note acknowledging this deviation as a required refinement, the task silently alters the defined statistical method, risking traceability failure.

Task T015 enforces the ≤10,000 composition cap but does not specify the culling strategy (e.g., random vs. stratified). FR-003 requires stratification by alloy system. A naive cap implementation could violate the stratification requirement, silently weakening the data integrity constraint.
